reasons: Diggie reduces the impact of Tigreal's crowd-control engage.
Time Journey helps teammates survive Tigreal's setup window.

proof.summary: Tigreal's main threat is an AoE crowd-control engage, while Diggie's ultimate gives nearby allies cleanse and control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Tigreal's real engage.
Diggie stays close enough to protect the teammates Tigreal wants to catch.
failureCases: Tigreal baits Diggie's ultimate before committing.
Tigreal catches Diggie out of position.
Tigreal engages while Diggie's ultimate is on cooldown.

proof.summary: Diggie's bombs can help check space and reduce Tigreal's ability to start fights from hidden positions.
worksBestWhen: Diggie uses bombs to scout bushes and likely engage paths before the team groups.
failureCases: The team walks into Tigreal's engage range without checking vision first.

reasons: Valir can interrupt and push back frontline engage attempts.
Long-range poke makes it harder for Tigreal to start clean fights.

proof.summary: Valir can cleanse himself with his ultimate, reducing Tigreal's ability to lock him down during the engage.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps his ultimate available for Tigreal's commit instead of using it early.
failureCases: Tigreal catches Valir after Valir's ultimate has already been used.
Valir is flanked before he can react.

proof.summary: Valir's knockback can push Tigreal away and interrupt short-range engage attempts before Tigreal gets an ideal setup.
worksBestWhen: Valir maintains spacing and saves knockback for Tigreal's approach.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Tigreal commits.
Tigreal reaches Valir from fog or a flank angle.

reasons: Karrie can punish durable frontliners with sustained true damage.

proof.summary: Tigreal is a high-HP tank, while Karrie's true damage and Max HP-based damage can melt durable frontliners in extended fights.
worksBestWhen: Karrie attacks safely from the backline.
Karrie has enough time to apply sustained damage before being controlled.
failureCases: Tigreal catches Karrie before she can deal sustained damage.
Karrie is poorly positioned or cannot use Purify at the right time.

proof.summary: Karrie's tank-shredding pressure becomes more reliable after her core attack-speed and on-hit items are completed.
worksBestWhen: Karrie reaches her core item timing before decisive teamfights.
failureCases: The game is decided before Karrie reaches her core item spike.
Tigreal's team forces fights before Karrie has enough damage uptime.

reasons: Akai can disrupt Tigreal's setup timing and separate Tigreal from follow-up damage.

proof.summary: Tigreal wants to catch grouped enemies as a setter tank, while Akai can disrupt formations, push Tigreal away, and separate him from team follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Akai uses ultimate after Tigreal commits.
Akai pushes Tigreal away from the backline.
Akai has vision of Tigreal's engage path.
failureCases: Akai pushes enemies in the wrong direction.

reasons: Lunox can damage Tigreal's tanky build with magic penetration while using survival tools against engage.

proof.summary: Tigreal is a durable engage tank, while Lunox can damage tanky heroes with magic penetration and avoid some engage danger with survival tools.
worksBestWhen: Lunox has penetration or damage items.
failureCases: Tigreal catches Lunox before she reacts.
